_rubric: 0-2 each: correct_beyond_test, minimal, conventions, no_unrelated. sum 0-8.
_note: Blind scoring by the orchestrator (judge-of-record) for the Tier 2 expansion. Diffs presented by opaque per-app code (C/L/P + shuffled index, seed = app SHA); arm labels never consulted (mapping.json unread until after this file was written). One representative scored per byte-identical group; all codes in a group inherit the representative's score. Task inferred from diff content (blinding is on arm, not task). Task map: 1,2=feature 3=bug 4=behavior.

C01.c: camp task4 (behavior): guards Membership.involvements.key?, alerts via t('.invalid_involvement'), locale nested correctly; adds related controller test. clean.

C02.c: camp task4: same guard as C01, different self-test name. clean.

C03.c: camp task3 (bug): one-liner Current.user.rooms.first -> .last. exactly the intended fix.

C04.c: camp task1 (feature): excluding_ids scope on User + excluded_user_ids helper; applies to room+non-room scope; array+CSV handled; related tests. clean.

C05.c: camp task1: same shape as C04 (var 'users'); self-tests use assert_empty (slightly coarse but passing). impl correct.

C06.c: camp task2 (feature): @bots = User.active_bots.ordered + inline bots section reusing accounts/users/user partial. clean, minimal.

C08.c: camp task2: @bots + view section with hr separator + bot.svg icon label, reuses user partial. conventional.

C09.c: camp task4: params[:involvement].in?(keys) guard, no self-test. minimal, correct.

C10.c: camp task4: uses `rescue ArgumentError` for expected invalid-enum input (control-flow-by-exception, a mild smell vs the explicit guard the others use) + hardcodes the flash string in its self-test rather than I18n.t; also a nil/missing involvement param would set nil rather than alert. -1 conventions.

C11.c: camp task1: guarded scope.excluding_ids; tests assert id not included. clean.

C12.c: camp task1: inline scope.where.not(id:) (no named scope) + extra 'unchanged behavior' test. correct; inline vs scope is a wash.

C13.c: camp task4: key? guard + self test. clean.

C15.c: camp task2: @bots + section with labelled/aria-labelledby menu. accessible, clean.

C16.c: camp task1: excepted_user_ids naming + excluding_ids scope. clean.

C19.c: camp task2: @bots + h2 section + related controller test. clean.

C20.c: camp task2: @bots + h2 with t('.bots') locale + related test. i18n label, clean.

C22.c: camp task2: @bots + NEW _bot partial (avatar+name, no member controls) + hr + t('.bots'). largest of the bots diffs; separate partial defensible (bots shouldn't show member controls). clean.

C23.c: camp task1: excepted_user_ids + excluding_ids + thorough tests (multi-id CSV, includes-Kevin control). clean.

L01.c: lob task4 (behavior): removes `@story.is_deleted = false` from update so editing a deleted story no longer resurrects it; non-deleted unaffected. minimal, exactly right.

L02.c: lob task2 (feature): respond_to html/json; User#standing_summary (all-time counts) outside controller; preserves html + auth. re-indent inflates diff but necessary. clean.

L03.c: lob task1 (feature): InactiveUser.disown_with_cascade! model method; batch update_all over direct children by original author only; mirrors existing disown!. clean, correct.

L04.c: lob task2: respond_to; standing_summary uses comments.not_deleted (arguably more precise). correct.

L05.c: lob task3 (bug): after_action update_read_at only: [:unread] -> [:all, :unread]. the intended minimal fix (keeps :unread).

L09.c: lob task3: changes only: [:unread] -> [:all] — passes the failing (:all) test but DROPS read-marking from the :unread action, an unrequested behavior regression (intended fix keeps both, cf L05). -1 correct.

L13.c: lob task2: respond_to; standing_summary (all-time). correct, clean.

L16.c: lob task1: inline controller cascade, filters children by comment.user_id, disown! each. correct; per-child refresh_counts is slightly redundant but fine.

L17.c: lob task2: new UserStanding service (interval-scoped, mirrors HTML stats) with as_json. more code but a legit service object, consistent semantics. clean.

L18.c: lob task1: inline cascade; captures original_author_id BEFORE disown; direct children by original author. correct, careful.

L19.c: lob task2: User#standing_summary(since:) interval-scoped; respond_to. correct; self-test weaker (include-keys) but passes.

L20.c: lob task1: inline cascade with ActiveModel Boolean cast for truthy cascade; captures author id. correct, clean.

L21.c: lob task2: new UserStandingSummary service (all-time) with as_json. clean service object.

L23.c: lob task1: inline cascade + Boolean cast + 4 thorough request specs (no-cascade, cascade, other-author, grandchild). let->let! change is related to enabling the new tests. excellent.

L24.c: lob task1: inline cascade filtering children by @user.id (current user) rather than comment.user_id — works because disown is self-service (@user == author); functionally correct, mildly fragile identifier choice.

P01.c: pub task3 (bug): Article.find(id) -> Article.last_draft(id) in preview. the intended one-liner fix.

P02.c: pub task2 (feature): respond_to html/json; @tags.as_json(only: :name, methods: :articles_count); Tag#articles_count = published_contents.count. exactly name+count. clean.

P03.c: pub task4 (behavior): destroy guarded by @user != current_user (plus existing last-admin guard). minimal, correct.

P05.c: pub task1 (feature): permit :nickname, presence-|| default, setup form field + locale + 3 specs. complete, clean.

P06.c: pub task1: nickname_param helper + presence-|| default, BUT backend only — NO setup-form field/locale, so an operator can't actually choose the nickname through the setup UI the task is about. passes a param-posting test; incomplete feature. -1 correct.

P07.c: pub task2: render json: @tags, methods: :articles_count — includes all tag attrs + articles_count. 'at least name+count' is explicitly allowed, so compliant; slightly over-exposes columns. keep 2.

P08.c: pub task1: nickname_param helper + form field + locale. complete, clean.

P10.c: pub task1: inline presence-|| default + form field + locale + 3 specs. complete.

P13.c: pub task2: as_json(methods: :articles_count) (all attrs, allowed) + controller spec asserting published-only count. correct.

P14.c: pub task2: as_json(only: [:name], methods: [:articles_count]) + spec asserting exact array. clean.

P16.c: pub task1: separate permit(:nickname) helper (user_params unchanged) + form field + locale. slightly awkward double-permit but complete and correct.

P19.c: pub task2: @tags.map { name, articles_count } explicit hash + Tag#articles_count. exactly name+count, clean.

P20.c: pub task1: user_params.except(:nickname).merge(nickname: presence||default) — careful backend, BUT NO setup-form field/locale, same incompleteness as P06 (operator can't choose via UI). -1 correct.

P21.c: pub task2: @tags.map { name, articles_count } + controller spec + model spec (published-only). thorough, clean.
